# UC-14.9.16 · Port Scan Detection on OT Networks

## Description

Port scanning is a reconnaissance technique used by attackers to map OT network topology and identify exploitable services. OT security platforms detect port scan events with scanner and target component identification. Port scans in OT networks are almost never legitimate and warrant immediate investigation.

## Value

Port scanning is a reconnaissance technique used by attackers to map OT network topology and identify exploitable services. OT security platforms detect port scan events with scanner and target component identification. Port scans in OT networks are almost never legitimate and warrant immediate investigation.

## Implementation

Both platforms detect port scanning in OT networks. Alert immediately on any port scan event. Identify scanner source — is it an authorized vulnerability scanner or unknown? Correlate with network baseline. Block scanning source at network boundary if unauthorized. Escalate to SOC and OT security team.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) · `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:syslog` · Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:alert` (type_id="SCAN").
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Both platforms detect port scanning in OT networks. Alert immediately on any port scan event. Identify scanner source — is it an authorized vulnerability scanner or unknown? Correlate with network baseline. Block scanning source at network boundary if unauthorized. Escalate to SOC and OT security team.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nozomi sourcetype="nozomi:alert" type_id="SCAN*"
| table _time, name, src_ip, dst_ip, zone, risk
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Port Scan Detection on OT Networks** — Port scanning is a reconnaissance technique used by attackers to map OT network topology and identify exploitable services. OT security platforms detect port scan events with scanner and target component identification. Port scans in OT networks are almost never legitimate and warrant immediate investigation.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:syslog` · Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:alert` (type_id="SCAN"). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) · `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nozomi; **sourcetype**: nozomi:alert. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nozomi, sourcetype="nozomi:alert". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Pipeline stage (see **Port Scan Detection on OT Networks**): table _time, name, src_ip, dst_ip, zone, risk
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Run the search for a time range that should include both routine and change activity, then compare the same assets, flows, and alerts in the Cisco Cyber Vision UI and in Nozomi Guardian or CMC, matching whichever source the event came from. Reconcile severities, counts, and key fields in both tools.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Port scan alert log; scanner source identification; target analysis; network map overlay.

## SPL

```spl
index=nozomi sourcetype="nozomi:alert" type_id="SCAN*"
| table _time, name, src_ip, dst_ip, zone, risk
| sort -_time
```
